meta.summary: samblaster fast duplicate marking and structural-variant read extraction for read-id grouped SAM streams.
meta.description: samblaster fast duplicate marking and structural-variant read extraction for read-id grouped SAM streams.

test: samblaster --version 2>&1 | grep -F 'samblaster: Version 0.1.26' >/dev/null && samblaster --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'Tool to mark duplicates' >/dev/null && ldd $(command -v samblaster) | grep -F 'libstdc++' >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-samblaster-mark && cd /tmp/taf-samblaster-mark && printf '@HD	VN:1.6	SO:unknown
@SQ	SN:chr1	LN:1000
r1	99	chr1	100	60	10M	=	200	110	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
r1	147	chr1	200	60	10M	=	100	-110	TTACGTACGT	FFFFFFFFFF
r2	99	chr1	100	60	10M	=	200	110	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
r2	147	chr1	200	60	10M	=	100	-110	TTACGTACGT	FFFFFFFFFF
' > dup.sam && samblaster -i dup.sam -o marked.sam -q > run.out 2> run.err && grep -F "$(printf '@PG	ID:SAMBLASTER	VN:0.1.26')" marked.sam >/dev/null && grep -F "$(printf 'r2	1123	chr1')" marked.sam >/dev/null && grep -F "$(printf 'r2	1171	chr1')" marked.sam >/dev/null && grep -F '50.000%' run.err >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-samblaster-remove && cd /tmp/taf-samblaster-remove && printf '@HD	VN:1.6	SO:unknown
@SQ	SN:chr1	LN:1000
r1	99	chr1	100	60	10M	=	200	110	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
r1	147	chr1	200	60	10M	=	100	-110	TTACGTACGT	FFFFFFFFFF
r2	99	chr1	100	60	10M	=	200	110	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
r2	147	chr1	200	60	10M	=	100	-110	TTACGTACGT	FFFFFFFFFF
' > dup.sam && samblaster -i dup.sam -o nodup.sam --removeDups -q > run.out 2> run.err && grep -F 'Removed' run.err >/dev/null && ! grep -F "$(printf 'r2	')" nodup.sam >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-samblaster-unmapped && cd /tmp/taf-samblaster-unmapped && printf '@HD	VN:1.6	SO:unknown
@SQ	SN:chr1	LN:1000
unmap	4	*	0	0	*	*	0	0	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
clip	0	chr1	100	60	5S5M	*	0	0	ACGTACGTAA	FFFFFFFFFF
' > unmapped.sam && samblaster -i unmapped.sam -o marked.sam -u unmapped.fq --ignoreUnmated --minClipSize 5 -q > run.out 2> run.err && grep -F '@unmap' unmapped.fq >/dev/null && grep -F '@clip' unmapped.fq >/dev/null
